title: Spin-current model of electric polarization with the tensor gyromagnetic ratio
authors: Mariya Iv. Trukhanova, Pavel A. Andreev
abstract: The spin-current model of electric polarization of spin origin is developed for a magnetic structure with anisotropic tensor gyromagnetic ratio (g-factor). Three mechanisms of the magnetoelectric effect are proposed, caused by the symmetric Heisenberg exchange interaction, the Dzyaloshinsky-Moriya interaction, and the spin-spin interaction related to the odd anisotropy of the symmetric exchange interaction of magnetic ions via nonmagnetic ion. The dependence of electric polarization on the spin density and tensor g-factor in the generalized spin-current model is derived. New solutions for macroscopic electric polarization, that arise in the cycloidal and helicoidal spin orders and are caused by the non-diagonal components of the gyromagnetic ratio, are predicted. The extended of the spin-current model to including a tensor g-factor can be important for the magnetic ferroelectrics with heavy ions which take part in the formation of magnetoelectric effect.
summary: This paper extends the standard spin-current model of electric polarization to include the effects of an anisotropic tensor g-factor. It identifies three distinct mechanisms for the magnetoelectric effect: symmetric Heisenberg exchange, Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction, and a novel spin-spin interaction via nonmagnetic ions. The research demonstrates that the tensor nature of the g-factor allows for the emergence of new electric polarization components in complex magnetic textures like helicoidal spirals. This is particularly important for understanding multiferroic materials containing heavy ions where spin-orbit coupling is strong.
main_problem: The paper aims to generalize the spin-current model of electric polarization to account for the anisotropic tensor gyromagnetic ratio (g-factor) in magnetic structures.
main_result: The authors predict that the non-diagonal components of the tensor g-factor induce new, macroscopic electric polarization components in cycloidal and helicoidal spin orders that are absent in the isotropic scalar case.
method: The study utilizes the many-particle quantum hydrodynamics (MPQH) method applied to a microscopic Pauli-Schrölar Hamiltonian.
model_or_system: The model considers clusters of magnetic transition metal ions and diamagnetic oxygen ions, specifically focusing on multiferroic perovskite-like structures (e.g., rare-earth manganites) with noncollinear spin orders.
key_observables: Macroscopic electric polarization (P), spin current density tensor (Js), spin density, and torque density.
important_parameters: Tensor gyromagnetic ratio (g-factor), exchange interaction constants (Heisenberg and Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya), and spin density.
main_assumptions: The ions are treated as localized point-like quasi-particles in the crystal lattice, and the derivation focuses on the stationary state where the time derivative of momentum density is zero.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates a cycloidal spiral spin structure in the xy-plane with a wave vector along the x-axis.
paper_structure: The paper begins by defining the Hamiltonian and the problem of g-factor anisotropy, proceeds to derive the momentum and spin density balance equations using quantum hydrodynamics, analyzes specific polarization mechanisms (Heisenberg, DM, and Keffer-like), and concludes with predictions for specific spin geometries (cycloidal and helicoidal).
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (Color online) The cycloid spiral spin structure in the absence of an external magnetic field (17). The cycloidal structure lies in the xy-plane with the wave vector q = (q, 0, 0) which lies in this plane. Two projections of electric polariza- tion (20) and (21) appear according to the definition (19). One of them is determined by the non-diagonal component of g-factor.

FIG. 2. (Color online) The helicoidal spiral spin structure in the absence of an external magnetic field (23). The spin- density-wave state lies in the yz-plane with the wave vector q = (q, 0, 0) perpendicular to this plane. Two projections of electric polarization (24) and (25) appear according to the definition (19), and are determined by the non-diagonal com- ponents of the gyromagnetic ratio (g-factor).
